title: Resinous, Glassy-Jelly Brazilian Steak Pilaf
description: A rich and flavorful dish showcasing Brazilian flair, featuring tender fillet of steak in a glassy-jelly pilaf.
instructions: Heat 2 tbsp of oil in a pan over medium-high heat.
Season the fillet of steak with salt and pepper.
Sear the steak for 2-3 minutes per side, then set aside to rest.
In the same pan, add 2 tsp of dashi extract and cook for 1 minute.
Add 1 cup of water to the pan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 5 minutes.
In a separate pan, heat 2 tbsp of oil over medium heat.
Add 1 cup of uncooked rice and cook until lightly toasted, about 2-3 minutes.
Add 2 cups of water to the rice and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 15-20 minutes, or until the water is absorbed.
To assemble the pilaf, place a scoop of the cooked rice mixture into each scallop shell.
Slice the rested steak into thin strips and place on top of the rice.
Serve immediately and garnish with toasted sesame seeds, if desired.
